characters.name: 1971 Buick Riviera
characters.description: 1971 Buick RivieraThe1971 Buick Rivierais a rare personal luxury car with whichRay Vecchiohas a great fascination, some might even say obsession. This car was first purchased in Buffalo and the detective had gone to great extents to obtain it. He spent $5,000 of his savings on the car, which failed to impress his then-wife, Angie; it is hinted that this was a factor in their divorce. Ray has a fetish for all-original parts in the car; at one point he advises his friendBenton Fraserthat it took him seven years to find an original cigarette lighter, which therefore must never be used. Despite its age, the Riviera is still very powerful and fast. Ray takes considerable risks with it in the course of his duty, even using it as a roadblock before a speeding armour-plated truck (You Must Remember This). He was forced to blow it up with a single bullet in order to save his life as well as Fraser's inThe Man Who Knew Too Little, and also to take out the criminals chasing them. After this, he was deeply devastated and obliged to drive a car from the motor pool, which constantly broke down in the most inconvenient places. At the end ofThe Wild Bunch,Ray stated that the Riviera was irreplaceable as there were very few left on the streets, just as an identical green Buick Riviera drove past with a "For Sale" sign in its window. (The episode closed on him running after the car and shouting for the driver to stop.)

characters.name: Andie
characters.description: Andiewas a fourteen-year-old girl who had been taken in by social services and spent many years in various foster homes. It is unclear whether her parents were dead, had somehow abandoned her or mistreated her, but as a young adult, her brotherSidhad become her custodian, leading her to join a band of pickpockets, reselling the valuable items to pawn shops to provide for their basic needs. Sid had told Andie he was saving the money to afford a trip to Wyoming where their mother's relatives lived and owned a ranch. Andie believed this and looked forward to the day they would finally leave. In the winter of 1996, Andie stole an electronic organizer filled with sensitive information regarding the Senator from a murderer's coat pocket. Her life was under threat, and both ConstableBenton Fraserand DetectiveRay Vecchiobecame involved, trying to protect the siblings and locate the murderer. Fraser took Andie for a carriage ride and listened to her story, discerning the possible lie behind Sid's promise. When Sid admitted to Andie there was no ranch and no family in Wyoming, she ran away but was kidnapped by the man she had stolen the organizer from. Sid contacted the authorities, and a wild chase ensued. Sid was seriously wounded in the process. As he was about to leave in an ambulance, he told Andie they should leave Chicago and seek a healthier lifestyle. He also told Detective Vecchio he had led his sister to steal and she was not responsible for her actions. Vecchio responded he had a friend at the State's Attorney's office and would see to her defense. Andie was portrayed by Canadian actress Amy Stewart.

characters.name: Annie MacRae
characters.description: Annie MacRaeOccupationSchool TeacherNationalityAmericanStatusActiveActorMichele Scarabelli Sister Anne MacRae,known to her acquaintances as Annie, is a teacher at St. Fortunata's Catholic girls' school. As a teenager, she had a relationship withRay Vecchiountil her father caught them in the basement "doing a little bit of this and a little bit of that", and forbade her from ever seeing him again. As an adult, Annie became a lay nun, and went on to become a member of the senior staff of St. Fortunata's. Annie MacRae was portrayed by Canadian actress Michele Scarabelli.

characters.name: Elaine Besbriss
characters.description: Elaine BesbrissOccupationChicago Police DepartmentCivilian Aide, Police OfficerNationalityAmericanActorCatherine Bruhier Civilian Aide, Police Officer Elaine Besbrissis an American police officer from Chicago, Illinois. She first acted as Civilian Aide at the Chicago Police Department’s 27th precinct before graduating as an officer in September 1997 and being assigned to a new station. She was replaced byFrancesca Vecchioupon graduation.

characters.name: Charles Carver
characters.description: Charles CarverOccupationChemistry TutorNationalityAmericanStatusDetainedActorColm FeoreCharles Carveris a convicted arsonist who is also suspected of being involved in the deaths of several women. A master of psychological manipulation, he was arrested byRay Vecchiofor burning down a building on an undisclosed date. When his parole is granted, he immediately begins to exact revenge on Ray with his arsenal of psychological tactics, along with toys as clues. Charles Carver was portrayed by Canadian actor Colm Feore.

characters.name: Celine
characters.description: CelineOccupationStudentNationalityAmericanStatusMissingActorHeather McComb Celine(surname unknown) is a 16-year-old student at the St. Fortunata's Catholic girls' school. Coming from a troubled family background, she is rebellious and itinerant, often running off the school grounds to be tracked down by SisterAnnie MacRae.When Celine and her boyfriend, Todd, find a hidden vault in the school's basement containing a trove of priceless antiques and valuables, they take to selling the items for cash, hoping to raise enough to escape the school for good - although they know nothing of the nefarious purposes of their buyer. Celine was portrayed by American actress Heather McComb.

Celine was portrayed by American actress Heather McComb.

characters.name: Charlie Pike
characters.description: Charlie PikeOccupationFall GuyNationalityAmericanActorBrendan Kelly Charlie Pikeis a former professional boxer who was recruited by a doctor to step in front of cars for a medical insurance scam. His six-year-old daughter, Lucy, who has already lost her mother to illness, appeals toBenton Fraserto prevent Charlie from getting hurt again. Through the intervention of Fraser andRay Vecchio, Charlie manages to detach himself from the scammers before he comes to fatal harm. Charlie Pike was portrayed by Irish actor Brendan Kelly.

characters.name: Charlie Wong
characters.description: Charlie WongOccupationCrime BossNationalityChineseStatusIncarceratedActorJoel de la Fuente Charlie Wongis a Tong boss seeking to establish a toehold in the Chinatown district of Chicago. His efforts are hampered by his activist neighbor,Mr. Lee, who resists his presence and rallies other local business owners to prevent Wong from taking over their enterprises. In retaliation, Wong orders Mr. Lee's son, David, kidnapped; butBenton FraserandRay Vecchioare within earshot of the kidnapping and resolve to bring Wong to justice. Charlie Wong was portrayed by Filipino-American actor Joel de la Fuente.

characters.name: Herb Colling
characters.description: Herb Collingwas a senior citizen of Chicago who was bent on exercising vigilante justice against muggers and vandals in his neighborhood. When he took the law into his own hands, Benton Fraser was obliged to put a stop to his acts of vigilantism by imposing upon him the responsibility for deciding who deserved punishment. Herb Colling was portrayed by American actor Carl Gordon.

characters.name: Del Porter
characters.description: Del PorterNationalityAmericanActorRyan PhillippeDel Porteris the teenage son ofWilliam Porter, a former bank robber and author. On Christmas Eve 1994, Del was seeking to buy a new pen for William when he quite literally ran into him during a bank robbery, in which William was acting as the getaway driver. Though Del was capable of positively identifying his father, he refused to cooperate with the police and risked a reprisal from William's partners. He expressed toBenton Fraserthat all he truly wanted from his father was to be in his life, contradicting William's wish to destroy himself and leave all the stolen money with Del. Del Porter was portrayed by American actorRyan Phillippe.

characters.name: Denny Scarpa
characters.description: Denny ScarpaOccupationCard PlayerNationalityAmericanActorStephanie Romanov Denny Scarpa,often known by her nickname "Lady Shoes," is a cardsharp and escape artist in custody of the Chicago Police Department until she is approached by FBI agents who want to use her gambling skills to gain access to a criminal kingpin. To avoid being exploited, she seducesBenton Fraserto grant her asylum in the Canadian consulate. Denny Scarpa was portrayed by American actress Stephanie Romanov.

characters.name: Diefenbaker
characters.description: DiefenbakerOccupationDetection DogDiefenbakeris a wolf hybrid from coastal British Columbia. While posted at the Prince Rupert detachment in 1992, ConstableBenton Fraserfound himself in a dangerous situation and met the animal as he held on to an ice floe drifting along the shore. The man began speaking to the wolf out of sheer boredom, and Diefenbaker dove into the Prince Rupert sound's icy waters to save him. This resulted in the wolf's partial deafness after his eardrums burst from the cold. Fraser trained the animal and named him after Canada's 13th Prime Minister, John Diefenbaker. Diefenbaker became Fraser's faithful police dog and followed him across the North where he learned to read lips in both English and Inuktitut. Diefenbaker does not wear a collar and doesn't seem to be officially registered as aRCMPdetection dog, but he is accepted and readily put to good use when needed by the Force. Diefenbaker is very loyal to Fraser and although they occasionally give each other the cold shoulder, the wolf has accompanied Fraser to Chicago where, to the constable's despair, he has become much of a city dog, developing a taste for fast food and even has his own checking account, which Fraser is careful to never disturb. Diefenbaker has had several run-ins with animal control, but DetectiveRaymond Vecchioforged a "wolf permit" that would allow him to stay in the city. During his first months in Chicago, the wolf often stayed in Fraser's apartment with his minder Willie, a twelve-year-old purse snatcher, until he escaped and was almost put to sleep for not only biting an animal control officer but also Fraser. An illegal operation running from the animal control office was discovered, allowing Diefenbaker to save the lives of numerous other dogs, including the pups he fathered with a husky named Maggie.

characters.name: Eric
characters.description: EricNationalityTsimshianActorRodney Arnold GrantEricis a Tsimshian man from northern British Columbia who came to Chicago in the winter of 1996 to retrieve two native masks from the Field Museum of Natural History. The masks had originally been confiscated by an Anglican priest in 1879 in order to convert the Aboriginal people to Christianity by taking their religious symbols from them. The official story was that the masks had been sold as artifacts and legitimately purchased later to be exhibited to the public, but the Tsimshian people had a different perspective on it and believed they had been wronged. They demanded the masks be returned to them, and since nothing was being done, Eric took his teenage nephewDavid Kitikmeotwith him to Chicago to steal the masks from the museum. David was caught, and Eric turned to his friendBenton Fraserfor help, lying to him about his real intentions. It is hinted that Eric had once saved Fraser's life and had also initiated him to the sweat lodge ceremony at the age of ten. Eric and his whole family took up residence in Fraser's apartment during their stay and introduced DetectiveRaymond Vecchioand the building's tenants to their customs. When it became obvious that the masks were fake and that the museum curator and a Canadian government representative had taken the real masks to sell them (the masks were worth over a million dollars), Eric managed to retrieve both sets, take the original masks and return to his people, leaving the fake set behind. Constable Fraser let them leave without too much fuss, feeling the Tsimshians had possibly been wronged in this affair, as the people of the First Nations had often been throughout history.

characters.name: Father Behan
characters.description: Father BehanOccupationCatholic PriestNationalityIrishActorShay DuffinFather Behanis a Catholic priest and pastor of St. Michael's Catholic Church in Chicago. Originally from Belfast, Ireland, he becomes acquainted withBenton FraserandRay Vecchio, both of whom come to him with cases involving his acquaintances and confessions of their past deeds. Father Behan was portrayed by Irish actor Shay Duffin.

characters.name: Benton Fraser
characters.description: Benton FraserOccupationRoyal Canadian Mounted Police ConstableDeputy Liaison OfficerNationalityCanadianActorPaul Gross Deputy Liaison Officer Benton Fraser(born 1962) is a constable of theRoyal Canadian Mounted Police. Born and raised in the Northwest Territories, he works as Deputy Liaison Officer at the Canadian consulate in the American city ofChicago. He lives in a dangerous neighbourhood at apartment 3J at 221 East Racine and later at the consulate itself. Fraser's strange habits, such as tasting evidence and holding the door for anyone and everyone, add hilarity and whimsy toDue South.

characters.name: Benton Fraser

characters.name: Caroline Fraser
characters.description: Caroline FraserNationalityCanadianStatusDeceasedActorMartha Burns Caroline Fraser(née Pinsent) wasRobert Fraser's wife andBenton Fraser's mother. She was assassinated by a weapon smuggler namedHolloway Muldoonin 1968, when her son was six years old. Benton Fraser was later sent to live with his grandparents since his father could not raise him on his own. He would only find out how his mother died in 1999. Robert Fraser and his friendBuck Frobisherwere both in love with Caroline Pinsent and had a shooting match to determine who would be allowed to court her.

characters.name: Caroline Fraser

characters.name: Martha Fraser
characters.description: Martha FraserOccupationPrimary School TeacherLibrarianNationalityCanadianStatusDeceasedActorFrances Hyland Librarian Martha FraserwasRobert Fraser's mother andBenton Fraser's grandmother. A resident of the Canadian territories, she worked as a traveling librarian with her husband George and traveled across the North, moving from one Inuit community to the next. WhenCaroline Fraserwas murdered in 1968, Benton Fraser was sent to live with his grandparents. Martha Fraser raised her grandson to be chivalrous and refused to indulge him so he would be tough, responsible and courageous.  Among other skills, she taught him to box.[1]

characters.description: Martha FraserOccupationPrimary School TeacherLibrarianNationalityCanadianStatusDeceasedActorFrances Hyland Librarian Martha FraserwasRobert Fraser's mother andBenton Fraser's grandmother. characters.name: Robert Fraser
characters.description: Robert FraserOccupationRoyal Canadian Mounted Police SergeantNationalityCanadianStatusDeceasedActorGordon Pinsent Robert Fraser(1937-1994) was aRoyal Canadian Mounted Policesergeant, and ConstablesBenton FraserandMargaret MacKenzie's father. In the spring of 1994, Sergeant Fraser was shot and killed under mysterious circumstances, leaving his son to search for his murderer, a search which led the young man to the city of Chicago. Fraser Sr. was portrayed by Canadian actorGordon Pinsent.

characters.name: Robert Fraser

characters.name: Buck Frobisher
characters.description: Buck FrobisherOccupationRoyal Canadian Mounted PoliceSergeantNationalityCanadianActorLeslie Nielsen Sergeant Buck Frobisheris a sergeant of theRoyal Canadian Mounted Police. He served withRobert Fraseras his partner in the RCMP for many years, and was Fraser Sr.'s best friend. ConstableBenton Fraserencounters Frobisher in Chicago in the fall of 1994 through his daughterJuliewho is worried about him. Frobisher is running from an old enemy, but in the end rises to the challenge of defending his dignity. Frobisher reappears later, mainly in a comedic role, notably in his tendencies toward flatulence, and toward making overly-dramatic speeches.
